name: Ehime Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the lush mountains and farmland of Ehime
the mikan (mandarin oranges) and other citrus that are Ehime's signature crop
purity, peace and the prefecture's harmonious development

description: A green field with broad horizontal yellow bands at top and bottom, charged at the centre with a white five-petalled mikan blossom enclosing a stylised hiragana え.
history: Ehime's prefectural symbol was adopted on 12 December 1952 and the flag, combining the symbol with horizontal yellow bands on a green field, was officially adopted on 28 November 1969.

features.symbolism: yellow bands above and below a broad green field, evoking citrus fruits ripening among Ehime's groves

features.role: Ehime prefectural emblem (mikan blossom encircling a stylised hiragana え)
features.symbolism: a five-petalled mikan (mandarin orange) blossom in white, encircling a stylised hiragana え (e) of 愛媛 (Ehime); the mikan flower is the prefectural emblem and recalls Ehime's identity as Japan's foremost producer of citrus.
